title: Brazil as the Empirical Center of SignWriting
subtitle: Documented institutional density, research opportunity, and why Brazil matters
summary: Brazil is the clearest documented empirical center for SignWriting use, research, and institutional density.
synopsis: Brazil is the first major empirical anchor of the Sutton SignWriting evidence record. The strongest existing Brazil material supports a disciplined brief: Brazil should be treated as the clearest documented empirical center for SignWriting rather than as a side example or supporting anecdote.
cover_image_alt: A warm research map centered on Brazil with SignWriting-like symbols, institutional records, classroom materials, and dense evidence-network anchors.

slides.title: Why Brazil Matters
slides.summary: Brazil is the strongest documented empirical center for SignWriting in the current public record.

image_prompts.concrete: Scholarly map slide highlighting Brazil with surrounding evidence markers for universities, publications, communities, and SignWriting materials
image_prompts.abstract: A dense empirical center marked by layered documentary evidence

bullets: Evidence of real use rather than mere possibility
A foundation for future research questions
slides.speech: Brazil deserves separate treatment because it is not just one example among many. In the current public record, it is the strongest documented empirical center for SignWriting, with educational, institutional, scholarly, and community-facing evidence that makes the system visible in practice.
slides.notes: Keep this as an evidence claim, not a claim about universal adoption.

slides.title: The Documented Floor
slides.summary: The available record establishes a documented floor for Brazilian SignWriting activity, not a complete census.

image_prompts.concrete: Academic evidence slide showing a floor line labeled documented minimum, with additional unknown activity above it
image_prompts.abstract: A solid foundation with open space above for future documentation

bullets: Known evidence should not be treated as the whole field
slides.speech: The Brazil evidence should be read as a documented floor. It shows enough to establish real depth, but it should not be mistaken for a complete census of every classroom, project, publication, or community practice.
slides.notes: This slide protects the claim from overgeneralization.

slides.title: Institutional Shape
slides.summary: Brazil matters because the evidence is not only individual enthusiasm; it includes organized educational and scholarly activity.

image_prompts.concrete: Structured slide showing educational programs, scholarship, public resources, and community use connected in Brazil
image_prompts.abstract: Multiple institutional threads crossing into a stable evidence knot

bullets: Educational use gives the record depth
slides.speech: The institutional shape matters because adoption evidence becomes stronger when it is not only individual enthusiasm. Brazil shows organized educational and scholarly activity, and that makes it a serious anchor for future research.
slides.notes: Name institutional density without implying that every institution has the same role.

slides.title: Scientific Meaning
slides.summary: Brazil turns SignWriting from an abstract proposal into a researchable writing practice with documented users and materials.

image_prompts.concrete: Research slide showing documented SignWriting materials becoming research questions about literacy, reading, pedagogy, and corpus work
image_prompts.abstract: Documented practice opening into research pathways

bullets: Reader and teacher perspectives still matter
The Brazil record gives future work a real base
slides.speech: Scientifically, Brazil matters because it turns SignWriting from an abstract proposal into a researchable writing practice. The record does not answer every question, but it gives future literacy, reading, pedagogy, and corpus work a real base.
slides.notes: This is the bridge from evidence to research, not a substitute for research outcomes.

slides.title: Limits Of The Brazil Claim
slides.summary: Brazil is the strongest documented empirical center, but the record is still a documented floor rather than a complete national account.

image_prompts.concrete: Evidence-boundary slide for Brazil showing a solid documented floor, reader-centered evidence, and open boxes for current census, outcome evidence, resistance, and ordinary daily use
image_prompts.abstract: A strong evidence anchor with clearly marked open research and documentation boundaries

bullets: Reader evidence exists but remains limited
Resistance and dormant cases should be documented
slides.speech:
The Brazil claim is strongest when it stays precise. Brazil is the clearest documented empirical center for SignWriting in the current public record. That does not mean the record is a complete national census, that every 2018 program remains active at the same scale, or that all reader, teacher, and community perspectives are already known.

The bounded claim is still strong. Brazil gives the platform a serious empirical anchor, documented reader-centered questions, and a practical agenda for future evidence work: update the national accounting, verify current institutional use, document resistance and dormant cases, and separate ordinary daily writing practice from curriculum, publication, and archival activity.
slides.notes:
Close Brazil with confidence plus boundaries.

Emphasize:
- the claim is not weakened by being bounded
- Brazil is the strongest empirical anchor
- the next step is better documentation, not larger rhetoric

This gives the Brazil deck the same disciplined ending as the Korea deck.
